title: File Download Restrictions
excerpt:
Hello! I recently reached out via email, and received a kind and timely response. My company and I would love the capability to restrict user’s ability to download files from the “files” column. 
This way we can update k…

posts.author: KyDiemDigital (Ky Hargrave)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! I recently reached out via email, and received a kind and timely response. My company and I would love the capability to restrict user’s ability to download files from the “files” column.

This way we can update knowledge base items on the back end and no users have an outdated file saved on their laptop. Would anyone else benefit from this feature?

posts.author: storky (Stefan Stork)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! We have the same problem. We need this feature in order to keep the DSGVO (data security) safe!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@storky @KyDiemDigital

Until this is available, one alternative that might work for some use cases would be to have the files column exist in a “parallel” board. Automations are available to create the “parallel” items. Once the second files board has been setup, the files column can then be mirrored on the main board. With this configuration, anyone who does not have access to the second board with not even be able to see that the mirrored files column exists.

Jim - The Monday Man channel
Signup for a FREE Integromat account with this link and get a FREE hour with The Monday Man

posts.author: KyDiemDigital (Ky Hargrave)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for your idea! Would this allow users on the original board to still be able to view the files or no?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@KyDiemDigital

Only if they have access to the second board. For those that do not, it would be as though the column does not even exist, even if they are an owner on that board (the main board).

posts.author: storky (Stefan Stork)
posts.cookedHtml: We would still need the “viewers” to view the document in the file column, but restrict the ability to download it. When I change the column setting to “closed” (with the close icon), I think the solution should be in there. Just a thought.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@storky

The problem with restricting downloads is that it is not “real” security. If they can see it, they can do a screen grab or even just take a picture of the screen.

posts.author: storky (Stefan Stork)
posts.cookedHtml: @JCorrell that is very true

posts.author: sydneylynch (Sydney Lynch)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JCorrell I’m not sure how the parallel board would solve this problem? Unless I’m missing something, you’d still need to have access to the main board to view the information in that column on the parallel board. If you needed to have access to both, then what’s the benefit of the parallel board?

@KyDiemDigital Our use case sounds similar! We often use (or want) this sort of limitation around things like training or onboarding materials. For us it’s not as much about keeping the data secure, but more about making sure that people are going to the regularly updated place for information instead of something that is static/from when they downloaded it. For example, we don’t allow our users to download recordings of their training calls because the platform and technology are rapidly changing and we don’t want them to be referencing a video from 3 years ago to learn how to do something. Yes they could take screen shots, record their screen, etc to keep the information on their end but with a download restriction in place it at least ensures that the tools we provide didn’t allow them to do that.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@sydneylynch

It doesn’t. Maybe I need to wait until I’ve had my second cup of coffee before reading posts. Seems like I’m a little off my game this week. Good thing it’s Friday.

The files column does not allow permissions to be set so that you can select who can “view” the column, only who can “edit” it. I was originally thinking that people wanted to control who was able to view the column not control the DOWNLOAD of files from the column. I understand now. This is a completely valid request.

posts.author: sydneylynch (Sydney Lynch)
posts.cookedHtml: No worries - it happens to us all!

posts.author: briggle (Benjamin Briggle)
posts.cookedHtml:
For Shareable boards, I have attachments that I want to be able to share but still maintain full control over the files shared. This means I want the user to be able to view, but not download the content as I need to know who accessed the content.

My current use case is for a pre-release demo that we recorded which is in MP4 format. I want to know who accessed it and then prevent it from being downloaded. The intent is to tightly control the recording and prevent unauthorized access. When I launched with an external email, the 1st thing it did was download the video which is the last think I want to occur as I now have an uncontrolled file.

Another use case could be to restrict download on work in progress documentation. I work in a highly regulated space where pre-release documentation could be problematic with regulatory agencies if circulated before fully approved.

posts.author: JDatHRC (Joshua Dupuis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

The current Monday.com HIPAA compliance function seems to distribute control of documents down to the workstation level. My concern with this function is that secure documents are forced to be distributed to dozens of machines versus keeping the file in Monday.

I would like to see an option that allows people in a workspace to read a document in Monday and restrict the downloading of the document. When someone views the document, they should be tagged as one of the viewers for auditing purposes.

This could be an optional setting.

Thanks.

posts.author: CeciliaQ (Cecilia)
posts.cookedHtml: I need file download and print restrictions too

posts.author: C.Skinner (Chance Skinner)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is also a feature we need at my company.

Our use case is for proposal collaboration and writing. We want all the supporting documents as well as the final submitted version of the proposal to be available for a large group of our company to be used for future proposal writing. However, we do not want users to be able to download that sensitive information for obvious reasons.

I dI think this feature would be highly useful for several use cases and should be looked into.
